summary: Merged batch decompilation of pointer-math-heavy functions across game-mode results/regclear, ground stage/material code, and item Kamex/ZGShell code. The diff replaced many `/// #` stubs with C, added typed prototypes, and introduced partial layout knowledge such as `Ground.xC0`, `grCastle_GroundVars3`, `itGShell_HurtInit`, and `itKamexAttributes`. The important review lesson was negative: maintainer ribbanya merged but explicitly warned that pervasive raw pointer arithmetic from the Claude workflow creates technical debt, and said `M2C_FIELD` would be preferred over raw `u8*` math when proper struct fields are not available. Evidence on exact match status is mixed: the PR body claimed 18 functions and 100% objdiff, while the decomp.dev bot comment reported 14 new matches plus 5 partial improvements, including several functions below 100%.

key_files.role: Added `fn_8017F14C`, exposed `lbl_80472D28_t.x10A`, and used a local argument-layout struct at offset 0x98.
Added `fn_80179F84` JObj child traversal and `fn_8017A9B4` image descriptor setup using raw result-data offsets.
Added `grCastle_801CD960` DynamicsDesc loop and `grCastle_801CF750` collision/offset update.
Added `grMaterial_801C9490`, `grMaterial_801C9604`, `grMaterial_801C9698`, updated `grMaterial_801C9470` signature, and centralized overlay offset through `grMaterial_GetOverlay`.
Added `fn_802010A4`, scanning a 17-entry table with 0x18-byte stride via raw pointer math.
Added `DynamicsDesc` include usage, `grCastle_GroundVars3`, `Ground.xC0`, and `castle3` union member.
Added `itGShell_HurtInit`, expanded `itGShell_ItemVars`, added `itKamexAttributes`, and changed pokemon item-var `x68`/`x6C` from int to f32.
Added Kamex init/motion/physics functions and replaced one `M2C_FIELD` access with `ip->xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.x68`.
Added five ZGShell functions for init, entered-air state, collision handling, and hurt capsule setup; replaced one `M2C_FIELD` with `zgshell.xE0C`.

what_changed: Replaced `/// #` placeholders with C implementations for functions named in the PR body: `fn_80179F84`, `fn_8017A9B4`, `fn_802010A4`, five `itzgshell` functions, three `itkamex` functions, `fn_8017F14C`, three `grmaterial` functions, and two `grcastle` functions.

In `gmregclear`, split padding in `lbl_80472D28_t` to expose `s16 x10A`; `fn_8017F14C` now reads an argument field at offset 0x98, clamps values above 9999, multiplies by `x10A`, and returns 0 unless `gm_8016AE38()->x24C8.x4_7` is set.

In `gmresultplayer`, changed several result globals to `extern`, added a bitfield layout for `lbl_8046E3AC`, stored up to four sibling `HSD_JObj*` values into `lbl_8046E39C`, and used `HSD_ImageDesc` plus `lb_800121FC` to populate images from result-data offsets.

In `grcastle`, added a typed `DynamicsDesc x1C[12]` layout under `grCastle_GroundVars3` and looped over it in `grCastle_801CD960`; `grCastle_801CF750` still uses raw byte offsets to adjust a float at `Ground+0xD0` or `Ground+0xE0` depending on `arg1`.

In `grmaterial`, changed command callback types from `HSD_JObj*` to `Item_GObj*, CommandInfo*`, added `Ground.xC0`, wrote a command-derived value into `gp->xC0`, and initialized/updated `ColorOverlay` state through a helper that still casts `Ground+0x40`.
In `grrcruise`, `fn_802010A4` checks a packed flag at `arg2[0x34]`, scans 17 table entries of size 0x18, initializes an entry when first seen, and increments its count.

In item headers, added concrete item-var and attribute layouts: `itGShell_HurtInit`, larger `itGShell_ItemVars` with a static assert changing from 88 to 120 bytes, `itKamexAttributes`, and f32 pokemon fields `x68`/`x6C`.

In `itkamex`, added initialization, action setup, and physics logic using typed item vars and attributes; one previous raw `M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C)` was replaced with `ip->xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.x68`.
In `itzgshell`, added shell setup, airborne transition, two similar collision handlers, and hurt capsule setup using a local `itGShell_HurtInit` copy before calling `it_80271534`.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increasing by 1576 bytes and matched data by 8 bytes, but also reported several functions as partial improvements rather than full matches.
smart_moves: The PR grouped functions with the same decompilation hazard, pointer math, into a single reviewable batch and explicitly flagged that match quality versus style might need discussion.

Some raw offsets were promoted to reusable type information instead of remaining anonymous: `lbl_80472D28_t.x10A`, `Ground.xC0`, `grCastle_GroundVars3.x1C[12]`, `itGShell_HurtInit`, `itKamexAttributes`, and f32 pokemon item-var fields.
Header prototypes were tightened from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete signatures, improving callsite typing for future decomp work.

`grMaterial_GetOverlay` centralized the repeated `Ground+0x40` color-overlay cast, which is better than duplicating the raw offset throughout the file even though a true `Ground` field would be preferable.
Using existing item-var paths such as `ip->xDD4_itemVar.zgshell.xE0C` and `ip->xDD4_itemVar.pokemon.x68` removed at least two older `M2C_FIELD` accesses and made later struct cleanup easier.

The `it_802DFF14` implementation copies the hurt init data to a local stack variable before conditionally changing offset/scale, preserving the source item-var data and matching the call pattern to `it_80271534`.
decomp_lessons: Do not treat pervasive raw `u8*` pointer math as an acceptable end state. The maintainer explicitly called it technical debt because it produces code that must be rewritten and does not contribute enough to struct definitions.
When a real struct field is not yet known, project feedback in this PR says `M2C_FIELD` is preferred over ad hoc raw pointer arithmetic; when the field is known, add it to the struct/header instead.
Partial layout extraction is still useful: even small fields like `Ground.xC0`, `lbl_80472D28_t.x10A`, or `pokemon.x68`/`x6C` can eliminate scattered casts and unlock cleaner future functions.
For stage-specific `Ground` unions, add narrowly scoped layout variants when evidence supports them, as with `grCastle_GroundVars3` for a DynamicsDesc array.
For item decomp, use `ip->xDD4_itemVar.<item>` and item-specific attribute structs to replace raw `ip+E..` offsets whenever possible.
For matching, `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(16)` were used in several new C functions; keep stack padding explicit when objdiff requires it.
Packed bitfields should use existing named bitfields when available, such as `cd->x34_flags.b1234`; raw flag checks like `arg2[0x34]` should become follow-up type work.

Be cautious with PR-body match claims. Here the body says all functions were 100% in objdiff, but the visible decomp.dev report listed several partial matches and a `.bss` regression, so future records should preserve both pieces of evidence rather than assuming final exactness.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-semantic functions keep address-based names such as `fn_8017F14C`, `fn_80179F84`, `fn_8017A9B4`, and `fn_802010A4`.
Subsystem functions keep prefix conventions: `grCastle_`, `grMaterial_`, `grRCruise_`, `itKamex_`, `itZrshell_`, `itZGShell_`, and `it_2725_Logic11_...`.
Unknown struct fields preserve offset-style names such as `x10A`, `xC0`, `x58_hurtInit`, `x68`, `x6C`, with padding named by offset like `pad_74` or `pad_10C`.
Stage-specific ground layouts can be numbered when multiple variants coexist, as shown by `grCastle_GroundVars3` and the `castle3` union member.
Bitfields use existing offset/bit naming patterns such as `x0_4`, `x10_flags.b4`, `x10_flags.b6`, `xE1C_b0`, and `xDC8_word.flags.x1F` until semantics are known.
Temporary local layout structs may be named after the function, e.g. `fn_8017F14C_arg`, but should be promoted or renamed if reused.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used raw byte-base calculations to match table layouts: `slot * 0xA8`, `slot * 0x18`, `lookup << 1`, `i * 0x18`, and `idx * 16`.
Used explicit typed loads/stores through casts such as `*(u16*)`, `*(s16*)`, `*(s32*)`, and `*(f32*)` where the surrounding struct was not modeled.
Kept separate locals such as `child`, `next`, and `HSD_JObj** p` in `fn_80179F84` to reproduce sibling traversal and store ordering.
Used stack padding macros in newly decompiled functions: `PAD_STACK(16)` in `fn_8017F14C`-adjacent code paths and `grCastle_801CF750`, and `PAD_STACK(8)` in `it_802DE0F0`.
For `fn_8017F14C`, matched the clamp-before-multiply sequence: copy `p->x98`, cap at 9999, multiply by `lbl_80472D28.x10A`, otherwise return 0.
For `it_802DFF14`, copied `itGShell_HurtInit` by value, conditionally modified the copy, then cast it to `HurtCapsule*` for the call.
For `grMaterial`, the inline overlay accessor likely helped avoid repeating a raw cast while preserving the same effective address calculation.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Could merge, but asked the author to address pointer-math generation in the workflow; said this was more pervasive than other Claude submissions and creates unnecessary technical debt by writing code that needs rewriting and not contributing to struct definitions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Observed no application of `M2C_FIELD` and said `M2C_FIELD` would be greatly preferred over raw pointer arithmetic.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Reported +1576 matched-code bytes and +8 matched-data bytes, with 14 new matches, 5 improvements in unmatched items, and 1 `.bss` regression in `gmresultplayer`; this does not fully support the PR-body claim that all functions were 100% matched.

review_feedback.author: agent
review_feedback.feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice; feedback came through issue comments.

follow_up_queries: Find remaining raw `u8*` pointer arithmetic introduced here and decide whether each offset should become a struct field or `M2C_FIELD`.
Model the layout behind `lbl_8046E1B0` so `fn_8017A9B4` can stop using result-data offsets like 0x224, 0x24B0, and 0x24C0.
Define a real grRCruise table-entry struct for the 17 entries of size 0x18 currently reached through `gp->gv.pad_0 + 0x6C`.
Replace `grMaterial_GetOverlay`'s `Ground+0x40` cast with a named `Ground` field if the `ColorOverlay` layout is confirmed.
Investigate the `grCastle_801CF750` update at `Ground+0xD0`/`idx * 16` and add fields for the two float targets if valid.
Check whether `fn_8017F14C_arg` is a known shared type rather than a function-local placeholder layout.
Reconcile the PR title/body claim of 18 fully matched functions with the bot report that lists 13 fully matched functions plus 4 partially matched functions and data items.
Audit future Claude-generated decomp PRs for the same pointer-math pattern before merge.
